events.event_description: Townsend Harris, the first U.S. chief of mission to Japan, presented credentials as Minister Resident, establishing the U.S. Legation in Japan.

evidence.quote: Townsend Harris, Minister Resident. Appointed: 1859-01-19. Credentials presented: 1859-11-05. Presented recall on 1862-04-26.

events.event_description: Luke Edward Wright presented credentials as Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary, marking the elevation of the U.S. mission in Japan from a Legation to an Embassy.

evidence.quote: Luke Edward Wright, Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1906-01-25. Credentials presented: 1906-05-26. Left Japan 1907-08-13.

events.event_description: Japan declared war on the United States; Ambassador Grew was interned and eventually left Japan in June 1942, ending U.S. diplomatic representation.

evidence.quote: Japan declared war on the United States on (local time) 1941-12-08. Note: Grew, having been interned, left Japan on June 25, 1942.

events.event_description: Embassy Tokyo was reestablished on April 28, 1952, with Ambassador Murphy in charge pending presentation of his letter of credence, restoring full U.S. diplomatic representation in Japan.

evidence.quote: Note: Embassy Tokyo was reestablished April 28, 1952, with Murphy in charge pending presentation of his letter of credence.
